frameworks.name: generative adversarial network
frameworks.one_line: generator-discriminator dynamics frame visual imagery in brain

frameworks.name: two-pathway visual processing
frameworks.one_line: conscious and nonconscious pathways instantiate adversarial interaction

frameworks.name: predictive processing
frameworks.one_line: internal predictions shape subjective visual experience

core_question: Can generative adversarial mechanisms explain intrusive visual imagery in PTSD and acute stress disorder?
key_assumptions: subjective imagery depends on generator-discriminator-like neural interaction
two visual pathways interact adversarially in healthy perception
imbalance between adversarial signals can produce pathological imagery
key_findings: proposes nonconscious pathway generates predictions influencing the conscious pathway
models intrusive imagery as overrepresentation of disturbing predicted content
interpretations: intrusive flashbacks reflect adversarial imbalance, not just memory dysfunction
subjective experience of mental disorders is tractable via AI-inspired adversarial models
